summary: Exceptional 1987 performance with scorching Crazy Fingers and a gorgeous China Doll, captured in an intimate open‑air setting despite no specific recording details.
review: Fans rave that this June‑21‑1987 show is one of the strongest ’80s era concerts, highlighting Jerry’s warm vocals on Crazy Fingers and a stunning China Doll that “steals the show.” The setlist feels varied and intimate, though reviewers give no concrete information about the source or fidelity of the recording.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews award five stars and praise the musical performance heavily; recording quality is not discussed, so rating leans on show excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewer comments about soundboard, audience mix, or clarity; focus is entirely on performance quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert is described as intimate and varied, with a strong first set and a second‑set China Doll that “steals the show,” reflecting a well‑balanced, day‑time college venue vibe.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s voice is warm and emotional; minor lyrical slip on Crazy Fingers noted but not detracting.
band_member_comments.Phil: Presence of a Phil song noted as part of the varied setlist.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s songwriting contribution mentioned (a Brent song in the first set).
band_member_comments.Drums: Brief reference to “drumz” before China Doll, but no specific evaluation.
